source.multipart_basis: Design-specific decomposition reflecting an FSG-style CFRP sandwich monocoque: separate upper and lower CFRP shells bonded at a split-line, discrete front bulkhead, main hoop and rear bulkhead, side impact structure, firewall, anti-intrusion plate, harness hardpoints, and metallic inserts. Part families correspond to SES-equivalency zones defined by FS Rules Ch T. No rulebook citations fabricated.

full_prompt:
Design a CFRP monocoque primary structure for a collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark 2026 electric-drive (EV) car, complying with FS Rules 2026 chapter T (Technical Regulations) and the Structural Equivalency Spreadsheet (SES) approach. The monocoque substitutes for the prescriptive steel space-frame primary structure; equivalency is demonstrated per FS Rules Appendix T.2. Vehicle total wet mass 240 kg excluding driver; driver 75 kg. Envelope: wheelbase 1600 mm, track 1200 mm, monocoque length 2200 mm, nominal cockpit cross-section 320 mm wide x 500 mm tall. Laminate schedule: CFRP skins 2 mm (outer) + aramid honeycomb core 25 mm + CFRP skins 2 mm (inner). Skin material: T800/epoxy twill fabric (E_eff 60 GPa, tensile 750 MPa, compressive 650 MPa, density 1540 kg/m^3). Core: Nomex aramid honeycomb 48 kg/m^3 (shear 1.45 MPa, density 48 kg/m^3). Mandatory SES equivalency targets vs steel space-frame baseline (prescriptive 1.0 inch x 0.095 wall 4130 chromoly): (a) side-impact equivalency: panel must absorb >= 7350 J at <= 40 g average on 300 kg mass; (b) front-bulkhead equivalency: front impact structure attached in front must transmit no more than 40 g peak to driver; (c) main-hoop + front-hoop equivalency: passing nominal rollover load of 6 kN axial on each hoop without yield; (d) harness bar tensile 13.24 kN per FS T.3.6.2. Load cases: (LC1) front impact 20 g on 315 kg (vehicle + driver) into 300 mm impact attenuator reacting at front bulkhead - monocoque front face must not rupture; (LC2) side impact 7 g on 315 kg at side-impact panel region; (LC3) rollover 6 kN axial on main-hoop peak mount while wheels pinned; (LC4) harness bar pull 13.24 kN applied at the harness attachment bolts into monocoque; (LC5) stiffness: torsional stiffness from +/-1000 N couple across front axle vs rear axle must exceed 2000 N*m/deg; (LC6) structural equivalency panel test (LC-SES): simulate the 3-point bending test defined in FS Rules Appendix T.2 (e.g. 3-point bend with span and load per the spreadsheet) and confirm energy/stiffness per spec. Pass/fail (per collegiate formula-class racecar 2026 + FS Rules Ch T): (R1) max CFRP skin Tsai-Wu failure index at LC1-LC4 ultimate must remain below 1.0 at factor-of-safety 1.5 applied; (R2) core shear under LC1 and LC2 must remain below 0.725 MPa (1.45 / 2.0 core FoS); (R3) torsional stiffness under LC5 must exceed 2000 N*m/deg; (R4) monocoque dry mass (all skins + core + inserts, excluding rollbars and other metallic hardware) must not exceed 45 kg; (R5) first torsional mode must exceed 25 Hz; (R6) harness bar pull-out under LC4 must not produce insert pull-through above 10 kN limit per FS Rules T.3.6.2; (R7) SES panel test equivalency (LC6) must meet the prescriptive baseline (bending stiffness E*I and energy absorption) recorded in the collegiate formula-class racecar SES spreadsheet. Thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling not primary. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of monocoque shell (sandwich layers as one solid), layup schedule, FEA per load case, SES equivalency tables, modal report.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
Delivered as a multi-part assembly reflecting the FSG monocoque physical build and the SES zone map: the outer CFRP sandwich is split into an upper shell and lower shell bonded at the cockpit split-line, with a front bulkhead co-bonded at the nose, a tubular main hoop welded into steel root shear plates bonded into the monocoque, a rear bulkhead terminating the cockpit aft, a side-impact structure zone (discrete CFRP panel zones upper and lower SIS per SES), a composite firewall aft of driver, an anti-intrusion plate inside the front bulkhead, harness hardpoints as metallic inserts at shoulder/lap/anti-submarine positions, and localized metallic inserts at suspension mounts. Interfaces include co-bonded (upper-lower shells, bulkheads, firewall, AIP), welded-into-insert (main hoop to root plate), and bolted (harness bar into hardpoint inserts). Final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC1 front impact). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design a CFRP monocoque primary structure for a collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark 2026 electric-drive (EV) car, complying with FS Rules 2026 chapter T and the Structural Equivalency Spreadsheet (SES) approach per FS Rules Appendix T.2.

Envelope: wheelbase 1600 mm, track 1200 mm, monocoque length 2200 mm, cockpit cross-section 320 mm wide x 500 mm tall. Vehicle wet mass 240 kg excluding 75 kg driver. Laminate: CFRP skins 2 mm + aramid honeycomb core 25 mm + CFRP skins 2 mm.

Materials: T800/epoxy twill (tensile 750 MPa, compressive 650 MPa); Nomex aramid honeycomb 48 kg/m^3 (shear 1.45 MPa).

Load cases: (LC1) front impact 20 g on 315 kg into 300 mm attenuator reacting at front bulkhead; (LC2) side impact 7 g on 315 kg at SIS region; (LC3) rollover 6 kN axial on main-hoop peak mount with wheels pinned; (LC4) harness bar pull 13.24 kN per FS T.3.6.2; (LC5) torsional stiffness from +/-1000 N couple across axles must exceed 2000 N*m/deg; (LC6) SES 3-point bend per Appendix T.2.

Pass/fail: (R1) CFRP skin Tsai-Wu index < 1.0 at FoS 1.5 across LC1-LC4; (R2) core shear under LC1/LC2 < 0.725 MPa (1.45/2.0); (R3) torsional stiffness > 2000 N*m/deg; (R4) monocoque dry mass <= 45 kg; (R5) first torsional mode > 25 Hz; (R6) harness pull-out under LC4 no insert pull-through above 10 kN per FS T.3.6.2; (R7) SES panel test equivalency met.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Structural Equivalency Spreadsheet is mandated by FS Rules and is not FEA; it tabulates E*I, E*A, and energy equivalence vs prescriptive steel baseline.

prompt.title: collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark 2026 CFRP Monocoque (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: CFRP skin + Nomex core sandwich monocoque, FS Rules Ch T + collegiate formula-class racecar SES equivalency to steel space frame. Decomposition: CFRP upper shell, CFRP lower shell, front bulkhead, main hoop + root plates, rear bulkhead, side impact structure zones, firewall, anti-intrusion plate, harness hardpoints, suspension/insert set. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under governing load case.

load_cases.name: SES_3pt_bend
load_cases.application: per collegiate formula-class racecar SES spreadsheet

parts.name: cfrp_shell_upper
parts.role: upper CFRP sandwich shell (roof over cockpit and upper SIS zone) co-bonded to lower shell at split-line

parts.name: cfrp_shell_lower
parts.role: lower CFRP sandwich shell (floor + lower SIS zone) co-bonded to upper shell and carrying suspension inserts

parts.name: front_bulkhead
parts.role: composite front bulkhead mounting the impact attenuator and reacting LC1 front-impact load into shell

geometric_constraints.interfaces: IA bolted forward; AIP bonded aft

parts.material_hint: CFRP sandwich with higher skin ply count

parts.name: main_hoop
parts.role: tubular steel main hoop welded into root shear plates bonded into monocoque; provides rollover equivalency

geometric_constraints.root_plate_pattern: bonded CFRP+steel shear plate per side

parts.name: rear_bulkhead
parts.role: composite rear bulkhead terminating cockpit and carrying harness shoulder hardpoints + rear suspension inserts

parts.name: side_impact_structure_zone
parts.role: discrete upper and lower side-impact panel zones of the shell with higher ply count per SES; must absorb 7350 J

parts.name: firewall
parts.role: fire-rated composite firewall aft of driver isolating HV battery/motor compartment

parts.name: anti_intrusion_plate
parts.role: anti-intrusion plate (AIP) bonded to aft face of front bulkhead

parts.name: harness_hardpoints
parts.role: metallic harness attachment inserts at shoulder (rear bulkhead), lap (lower shell), and anti-submarine (lower shell) positions

parts.name: suspension_inserts
parts.role: localized metallic inserts at four suspension pickup zones on lower shell

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: vehicle axes: X longitudinal (nose+), Y lateral, Z up; origin at nose bulkhead centerline

pass_fail_criteria.limit: >= prescriptive 4130 baseline for E*I and energy

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces (shell split-line, bulkhead bond lines, main hoop root plates, harness hardpoints, suspension inserts) <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC1 front impact).
Upper-lower shell split-line (IF1) bond peel stress under LC2 side impact must remain below adhesive-specific peel limit (design hint: 8 MPa for epoxy film adhesive).

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: layered shell (sandwich modeling) for monocoque; solids for metallic inserts and main hoop; connector elements for bonded interfaces

interfaces.mediated_by: co-bonded split-line joint (epoxy film adhesive + overlap lay-up)
interfaces.load_path: cockpit hoop-stiffness load path under LC2/LC5; peel/shear critical

interfaces.mediated_by: co-bonded nose bulkhead into lower shell (and into upper shell through perimeter tab)
interfaces.load_path: LC1 front-impact 61.9 kN reacted from IA into shell

interfaces.mediated_by: anti-intrusion plate bonded to aft face of front bulkhead
interfaces.load_path: penetration protection under LC1; AIP reacts into bulkhead skins

interfaces.mediated_by: main hoop welded to steel root plates bonded through both shells
interfaces.load_path: LC3 rollover 6 kN axial into shell via root plates

interfaces.mediated_by: rear bulkhead co-bonded to upper + lower shells
interfaces.load_path: cockpit closure + harness shoulder reaction load path

interfaces.mediated_by: firewall bonded to rear bulkhead and perimeter shell
interfaces.load_path: fire-barrier + HV compartment closure; minor load path

interfaces.mediated_by: harness hardpoint inserts bonded + through-bolted into lower shell and rear bulkhead
interfaces.load_path: LC4 13.24 kN harness pull reacted through insert pull-through (R6)

interfaces.mediated_by: suspension inserts bonded into lower shell at four pickup zones
interfaces.load_path: suspension loads + LC5 torsion couple reacted through insert zones

interfaces.mediated_by: SIS ply build-up continuous with upper and lower shells
interfaces.load_path: LC2 side-impact 21.67 kN absorbed in SIS zone

fem_check_policy.rationale: SES equivalency (R7) and torsional stiffness (R3) are defined on the closed monocoque; main-hoop rollover (R6/LC3) only reacts correctly via bonded root plates; harness pull-through (R6/LC4) is an insert-plus-laminate system; shell peel (IF1) is only meaningful on the assembled upper+lower shells.

notes.why_multi_part: FS Rules Appendix T.2 SES defines the monocoque as a zoned structure (front bulkhead, SIS upper/lower, main hoop, rear bulkhead, firewall, AIP, harness hardpoints) each with prescriptive baseline requirements; the physical build further splits the shell at a co-bond split-line.
notes.speculation_caveats: Zone layout and insert count are consistent with typical FSG practice; any explicit rulebook thresholds remain those carried from the original brief.
